text: Patient presents for first adult physical/wellness visit (first physical since college)
transcript_quote: first physical since... honestly, probably since college

text: Patient reports weight gain since starting a sedentary desk job
transcript_quote: Maybe that I have put on weight since my desk job started. Otherwise I feel fine.

text: No known chronic medical conditions (no hypertension, diabetes, asthma)
transcript_quote: Nothing. I have a pretty boring file, I think.

text: No prior surgeries or hospitalizations
transcript_quote: Never. Not even stitches.

text: No active medications; patient takes no regular prescriptions, vitamins, or supplements
transcript_quote: Nothing regular.

text: No known drug allergies
transcript_quote: None that I know of.

text: Family history unknown/none reported for heart disease, diabetes, cancer
transcript_quote: Honestly, nothing anyone has told me. My family is not big on talking about health.

text: Never smoker / no tobacco use
transcript_quote: Never. Cannot stand the smell.

text: Occasional alcohol use, one to two drinks about twice a month
transcript_quote: Occasionally — a drink or two if I am out with friends, maybe twice a month.

text: Occasional cannabis use (edibles a couple times a month); DAST-10 score 2, no functional problems
transcript_quote: some weed now and then. Edibles mostly, a couple of times a month.

text: Lives with three roommates (4 people at address); shared housing, stable housing
transcript_quote: I share a house with three roommates.

text: Employed full-time as a graphic designer, mostly remote; highly sedentary work
transcript_quote: Graphic design, full time, mostly remote. It pays fine. I just sit way too much.

text: Neighborhood violence exposure (shootings and an assault nearby); background stress affecting sense of safety walking home at night
transcript_quote: there have been a couple of shootings a few blocks over this year, and someone got jumped outside the corner store last month

text: Feels safe inside home but less safe walking home late; times runs for daylight

slots.safety: safe at home, unsafe walking at night

transcript_quote: In the house, yes. Walking home late, less so. I time my runs for daylight now.

text: Uninsured (no primary insurance)
GAD-7 anxiety screen score 1 (minimal)
transcript_quote: Your anxiety score was a one, basically minimal.

text: PHQ-2 depression screen score 2 (below threshold of concern)
transcript_quote: The depression screen came to a two — below the level we worry about

text: Occasional low mood ('gray Sundays') lasting no more than a day or two; sleep, appetite, energy normal
transcript_quote: Sometimes, sure. Gray Sundays. Nothing that sticks around past a day or two.

text: HARK domestic abuse screen negative (score 0); no partner violence, patient single
transcript_quote: No, nothing like that. I am single anyway

text: Cardiac exam: heart sounds regular
transcript_quote: Heart sounds nice and regular.

text: Pulmonary exam: lungs clear
transcript_quote: lungs are clear

text: Abdominal exam: no tenderness
transcript_quote: any tenderness when I press here?

text: Blood pressure 103/67 mm Hg (normal)
transcript_quote: Blood pressure is one-oh-three over sixty-seven, which is great.

text: Heart rate 69 /min
transcript_quote: Pulse sixty-nine

text: Respiratory rate 15 /min
transcript_quote: breathing fifteen a minute

text: Height 164.3 cm, weight 84.9 kg, BMI 31.45 kg/m2 (obese range)
transcript_quote: Height one sixty-four, weight eighty-four point nine kilos, so the body mass index comes to about thirty-one and a half, which crosses into the obese range

text: Obesity (BMI 31.45) identified and addressed
transcript_quote: which crosses into the obese range on paper

text: Lipid panel: Total cholesterol 110 mg/dL, LDL 36 mg/dL, HDL 51 mg/dL, triglycerides 117 mg/dL (excellent)
transcript_quote: Total cholesterol one-ten, the LDL — the bad one — around thirty-six, HDL fifty-one, triglycerides one-seventeen.

text: Patient currently exercises: runs about twice a week
transcript_quote: I run, when the weather and the neighborhood cooperate. Maybe twice a week.

text: Counseled to do 3 runs or brisk walks per week, 30-40 minutes, in daylight
transcript_quote: three runs or brisk walks a week, thirty to forty minutes, daylight hours

text: Counseled on dietary change — swap snacks rather than starve
transcript_quote: look at the snacking — swap it, do not starve

text: Counseled on standing desk / reducing sedentary time
transcript_quote: even alternating an hour up, an hour down changes how much you move

text: Nutrition class referral offered; patient declined for now, will try self-directed first
transcript_quote: I can refer you to a nutrition class if structure helps. / Let me try the do-it-myself version first.

text: Administered Td (adult tetanus-diphtheria) booster in left arm (overdue)
transcript_quote: Your tetanus booster is overdue — the adult Td

text: Administered influenza vaccine (trivalent split virus, PF) in right arm
transcript_quote: I would do the flu shot as well

text: Medication reconciliation completed; documented no active medications
transcript_quote: I will document officially that you have no active medications

text: Follow-up in one year for annual visit; return sooner if concerns arise
transcript_quote: You should come back — not in a decade, in a year. Sooner if anything comes up.

text: Counseled to contact clinic if neighborhood stress worsens (sleep changes, spiraling worry)
transcript_quote: if the neighborhood stress ever starts weighing heavier — sleep changing, worry spiraling — call us

text: Counseled that whole-body screening scans are not recommended; routine annual visit, vaccines, and screening are appropriate at his age
transcript_quote: Skip the scans — that is marketing, mostly.

text: Advised post-vaccination arm soreness expected; keep both arms moving
transcript_quote: Keep both arms moving today and tomorrow.

text: PRAPARE social needs assessment completed; overall stress level reported 'Not at all', housing stable, no transportation barriers
transcript_quote: The nurse also ran the mood questionnaires

text: Substance use assessment counseling: offered nonjudgmental support if cannabis use becomes a need
transcript_quote: If it ever starts feeling like a need instead of a choice, that is a conversation we can have here without judgment.
